text:
Cursor 今天开放了官方 TypeScript SDK 公测，把驱动自家编辑器、CLI 和网页版的智能体（agent，可自主执行编码任务的 AI 助手）整套打包交给开发者。装上 npm install @cursor/sdk 就能用。

调用方式很灵活。Agent 可以跑在本机，也可以塞到 Cursor 云端的独立虚拟机里。云端模式下每个 agent

RT @rileybrown: Learn 95% of Codex in 28 minutes

These are the 7 knowledge work capabilities...
inside Codex, the super-app

00:00 Intro
0…

GPT-5.5 shipped 7 days after Claude Opus 4.7.

Everyone picked a winner based on headlines.

I looked at every benchmark both labs published.

The real story isn't what most people are reporting: https://t.co/Alg3sfeILs

On the 10 benchmarks where both OpenAI and Anthropic report scores, here's the split:  

Claude Opus 4.7 leads on 6. GPT-5.5 leads on 4. 

 But the leads aren't random. They cluster by category.   

And that changes what "winning" means entirely. https://t.co/fnzbihvchk

Deep reasoning and code precision:

Claude Opus 4.7 
→ SWE-Bench Pro: 64.3% ✅ 
→ GPT-5.5: 58.6% ❌
→ MCP Atlas: 79.1% ✅ 
→ GPT-5.5: 75.3% ❌
→ GPQA Diamond, HLE (with and without tools), 
FinanceAgent v1.1: all Opus 4.7 ✅

When the task requires architectural thinking

Long-running agentic execution:

GPT-5.5
→ Terminal-Bench 2.0: 82.7% ✅
→ Claude Opus 4.7: 69.4% ❌
That's a 13-point gap. Not noise.
→ OSWorld-Verified: 78.7% vs 78.0% ✅
→ BrowseComp ✅
→ CyberGym ✅

When the task requires driving a terminal, recovering from errors, and

Token efficiency is where things get interesting.

GPT-5.5 uses 72% fewer output tokens than Opus 4.7 on the same coding tasks.

Fewer tokens means lower cost per task, even though GPT-5.5 costs $30/M output vs Claude's $25/M.

But Claude's time-to-first-token is roughly 0.5s vs

Tom's Guide ran 7 head-to-head tests. Claude won all 7.

OpenAI's own benchmark table shows GPT-5.5 leading on 14 categories. 

But that table includes tests where only OpenAI published a Claude score. 

Anthropic's own numbers tell a different story on several of those.

The

The definitive ranking doesn't exist. Here's what does:

→ Complex reasoning, code review, multi-file refactoring: Claude Opus 4.7
→ Agentic execution, terminal workflows, tool orchestration: GPT-5.5
→ Interactive speed: Claude
→ Cost at scale: GPT-5.5

The professionals

And this is the part nobody is talking about:
Both models ship with 1M-token context. 

Both are within a few points of each other on most tasks. The gap between them is smaller than the gap between a good prompt and a bad one.

The model is maybe 20% of your result. Your

We've moved to a world where image models are judged on accuracy - text, infographics, photorealism, rendering products / logos.

These models are great and have many valuable use cases.

But I sometimes find myself missing the age of aesthetics and random serendipity.

Love the hype ❤️🙌

Would really value your take after you try it, cuz builders shipping real products are the feedback we care about most

HDR renders change what motion design can even mean🤔

We're integrating HyperFrames into our open-source Claude Design alt — open-design, which got 3,000+ ⭐ on GitHub in 24h.

Shipping in hours.
https://t.co/aq2K0XbQcM
